reason: The statement applies because the text describes how the children of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD, leading to their suffering and oppression by the Midianites. The LORD sent a prophet and an angel to guide and deliver them from their troubles, showing a reaction to the disobedience of the people. The text portrays the LORD as responding with consequences to the actions of the Israelites, which can be interpreted as a form of anger or displeasure.
